purpose: Compact implementation scoreboard for the CTI/DWM/XDR product lanes. This is for the 10-minute coordinator loop; it should reduce management overhead and keep workers from shipping dashboard-only slices.

rows.currentStatus: Partial: auth/users exist; org APIs are actively being wired; buyer onboarding still lacks a crisp active-org console path.
rows.lastCommitOrHandoff: b750ac61 org-scoped DWM foundation; current dirty org route/schema work.
rows.nextCodeSlice: Add active-org selector/context, org create/list UI, and tenant propagation across TI/DWM routes.
rows.blocker: Need production probe proving dashboard routes use org context instead of default.

rows.currentStatus: Partial: invite acceptance API exists, but buyer-facing invite/resend/expire UI is not confirmed.
rows.nextCodeSlice: Build invite panel: create invite, copy/send link, accept invite, expire/resend, audit event.
rows.blocker: Email/support fallback and expiry policy need to be productized.

rows.currentStatus: Partial: membership routes exist; role enforcement across TI/DWM/admin actions still needs hard coverage.
rows.lastCommitOrHandoff: 8a389e7f membership routes; current dirty organization utility work.
rows.nextCodeSlice: Add role editor and enforce admin/analyst/viewer checks on watchlists, alerts, cases, support, and source controls.

rows.currentStatus: Partial: DWM watchlist API exists; shared team ownership, change history, and routing are not complete.
rows.lastCommitOrHandoff: 607db9e1 shared watchlist API; b750ac61 org-scoped DWM foundation.
rows.nextCodeSlice: Ship org-owned watchlist workspace with term type, owner, pause/resume, test match, last match, webhook route, and audit history.
rows.blocker: Needs durable version/audit model before multiple analysts edit the same list.

rows.currentStatus: Partial: adapters and source pages exist; active Telegram count is not yet an authoritative product KPI.
rows.nextCodeSlice: Add source KPI endpoint with enabled/failing Telegram sources, last successful check, next scheduled check, and parser status.
rows.blocker: Need live runtime proof inside freshness window.

rows.currentStatus: Partial: darkweb metadata adapters, policy boundaries, and source request lifecycle work exist; production-safe active counts are not proven.
rows.lastCommitOrHandoff: ec4289dc source request lifecycle; f16e8a0e control room.
rows.nextCodeSlice: Add metadata-only approval queue with active/failing onion source counts, last check, screenshots where safe, and blocked-reason fields.
rows.blocker: Must keep unsafe payloads, raw onion URLs, and credentials out of buyer/admin surfaces.

rows.currentStatus: Partial: alert provenance/repository work exists; demo fallback must be excluded from sellable buyer paths.
rows.lastCommitOrHandoff: 720414f2 alert provenance model; 3f55b12e alert repository adapter.
rows.nextCodeSlice: Prove real watchlist + real capture creates saved alert with evidence ID, source ID, dedupe key, match reason, and demoAlertsVisible=0.
rows.blocker: Need automated capture-to-alert smoke that fails if only demo data appears.

rows.currentStatus: Partial: DWM webhook files/tests are in progress; Discord delivery is operationally useful but not productized enough for buyers.
rows.nextCodeSlice: Put delivery controls on the DWM page: endpoint/Discord destination, redacted secret status, test send, payload preview, last deliveries, retry failed.
rows.blocker: Secret-safe config, retry semantics, and buyer-facing failure messages need final checks.

rows.currentStatus: Partial: DWM alert workflow and persisted ownership exist; one durable cross-product case object is not finished.
rows.lastCommitOrHandoff: 20f6563f persisted DWM case ownership; 70bcbb99 readiness workbench.
rows.nextCodeSlice: Persist cases over DWM/TI alerts with assign, note, escalate, suppress/false-positive, close/reopen, timeline, and delivery replay.
rows.blocker: Case schema must unify TI actor activity, DWM alerts, and delivery history.

rows.currentStatus: In progress: impersonation/admin-support files are dirty; no committed buyer-support readiness packet yet.
rows.lastCommitOrHandoff: Dirty handoff: api/src/handlers/adminSupport.ts, api/scripts/smoke-admin-support-contract.ts, impersonation route edits.
rows.nextCodeSlice: Ship safe support console: find org/user, view subscription/onboarding state, start audited impersonation, and file support actions.
rows.blocker: Needs strict audit trail and scoped auth before production use.

rows.currentStatus: In progress: admin audit utility and TI audit pages are dirty; audit events are not yet a stable coordinator KPI.
rows.lastCommitOrHandoff: Dirty handoff: api/src/utils/adminAudit.ts, TI audit route edits.
rows.nextCodeSlice: Add append/query audit events for org, invite, watchlist, source, alert, case, support, impersonation, and delivery actions.
rows.blocker: Need retention policy and event schema stability.

rows.currentStatus: Partial: /status and container health exist; no single product-progress record ties deploy health to CTI/DWM KPIs.
rows.lastCommitOrHandoff: 03d29b7c coordinator scoreboard; status work from recent thread handoffs.
rows.nextCodeSlice: Emit /api/product-progress or scraper SLO record every 10 minutes with commit, frontend/API/scraper health, and every KPI group in this table.
rows.blocker: Branch is ahead and multiple active dirty lanes must land before clean deploy proof.

antiDashboardRules: Do not ship metric cards unless there is a queue item that can be opened, reviewed, assigned, escalated, suppressed, replayed, or closed.
Do not show source counts without source health, last/next check, parser status, and a concrete operator action.
Do not show actor profiles without source links, changed fields, last refresh, next refresh, and a clear reason the profile changed.
Do not show alerts without evidence IDs, source IDs, timestamps, match reason, dedupe key, and delivery state.
Do not build webhook settings without test send, payload preview, retry history, and redacted secret handling.
Do not call a workflow autonomous unless the page exposes latest run time, changed records, next planned work, and failure/backoff state.
